items.title: Bursa Malaysia gains at opening on renewed buying in technology stocks
items.summary: The key index had opened 6.76 points up at 1,690.39. Among active stocks, Hong Seng Consolidated was flat at one sen, Tanco was down five sen to 15 sen and Top Glove slid one sen to 79.5 sen. Among top losers, Paragon fell 98 sen to RM2.29, Petronas Chemicals slid 47 sen to RM4.87,Ralco decreased 29 sen to 69 sen, Concrete Engineering lost 20 sen to RM4.00 and United Plantations was down 18 sen to RM31.32.

items.title: Police ramp up readiness with nearly 6,000 personnel for Negeri Sembilan election operations
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 15 — A total of 5,953 police personnel and members of other agencies will be deployed throughout the Negeri Sembilan state election period to ensure the democratic process proceeds smoothly and remains orderly. State police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said the figure comprises 4,845 senior police officers and rank-and-file personnel, while the remainder will serve as support personnel, including members of the People’s Volunteer Corps (Rela) and police volunteer reserve. Meanwhile, Alzafny said the police would continue to respect the right of all parties to conduct campaign activities as provided for under the law.

items.title: Rohingya say landowner built and offered illegal flats in Sungai Tekali
items.summary: KAJANG: Rohingya refugees who previously rented illegal flats in Sungai Tekali, Hulu Langat, claimed that the landowner had offered them the housing units since 2019. One of them, Husain Abdullah, said the flats were built in stages in 2018 and were completed in 2019. "There were two units rented by locals, while other units were rented by Rohingya residents.

items.title: Road rage claim in Kuala Lumpur: Driver says she was chased and car struck after honk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — A motorist’s account of an alleged road rage incident has gained traction on social media after she claimed her vehicle was struck by another driver following a brief warning honk. According to the New Straits Times , the incident was shared on Threads by a user identified as @whoisfaanyway, who alleged that a pickup truck driver entered her lane without noticing her vehicle. The user said she sounded her horn to alert the driver after noticing the vehicle moving into her lane.

items.title: US, Iran reach agreement to end war, signing set for Friday
items.summary: DUBAI/WASHINGTON: US and Iranian officials said on Sunday they had agreed on a framework to end their war, halt the US blockade of Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a preliminary pact that sent oil prices falling but leaves the fate of Iran's nuclear programme to further negotiations. "The Deal with the Islamic Republic of Iran is now complete," US President Donald Trump wrote on his Truth Social platform around 5.30pm ET local time in Washington (2130 GMT) on Sunday. Trump wrote.

items.title: Trailer driver with 15 summonses under probe after Karak highway crash kills baby girl and family
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The driver of a trailer involved in a fatal accident that claimed four lives at Kilometer 43 of the Kuala Lumpur-Karak (KLK) Expressway this evening was found to have 15 traffic summonses. Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman said police had also taken a blood sample from the 40-year-old man for toxicology analysis. He said the lorry driver was receiving further treatment for head and hip injuries.

items.title: Perak floods: 92 evacuated to two relief centres
items.summary: IPOH: The number of flood evacuees housed at temporary relief centres in Perak rose to 92 people from 33 families this morning, compared with 17 people from eight families last night. "The relief centre at SK Batu Hampar in the Larut, Matang and Selama district recorded an increase in evacuees compared with last night, housing 41 people from 14 families following floods affecting Kampung Batu 25, Kampung Berjaya, Kampung Batu 23 and Sungai Rotan. "Meanwhile, the relief centre at SK Beruas in the Manjung district recorded 51 evacuees from 19 families from flood-affected areas in Kampung Kilang, Taman Anggerik Permai and Kampung Pulau Kabung," it said in a statement.

items.title: Stepson of Norway's crown prince to hear verdict in rape trial
items.summary: Marius Borg Hoiby, 29, who joined the royal family when his mother Mette-Marit married Haakon in 2001, pleaded not guilty to the most severe accusations against him, including those of rape, while admitting to some lesser charges. One alleged rape took place in the basement of the crown prince's family home, the court heard. CASE HAS DENTED POPULARITY OF ROYALS Interest in the case was boosted by the contrast between the picture-perfect royal family and Hoiby's alleged actions as heard in court, said Ketil Raknes, an associate professor in political communication at the Kristiania University of Applied Sciences.

items.title: Flood evacuees in Perak surge overnight, 92 people from 33 families housed at two centres
items.summary: IPOH, June 15 — The number of flood evacuees housed at temporary relief centres in Perak rose to 92 people from 33 families this morning, compared with 17 people from eight families last night. “The relief centre at Sekolah Kebangsaan (SK) Batu Hampar in the Larut, Matang and Selama district recorded an increase in evacuees compared with last night, housing 41 people from 14 families following floods affecting Kampung Batu 25, Kampung Berjaya, Kampung Batu 23 and Sungai Rotan. “Meanwhile, the relief centre at SK Beruas in the Manjung district recorded 51 evacuees from 19 families from flood-affected areas in Kampung Kilang, Taman Anggerik Permai and Kampung Pulau Kabung,” it said in a statement.

items.title: Oil prices down more than 4pct after US-Iran deal to open Hormuz
items.summary: TOKYO: Oil prices plunged more than four per cent Monday after the United States and Iran announced they had reached a peace agreement to end months of a war that had roiled energy markets. West Texas Intermediate was down 4.39 per cent at US$81.15 a barrel in Tokyo after mediator Pakistan said the United States and Iran agreed to a peace deal and an "immediate and permanent" end to military operations on all fronts, including Lebanon. Soon after, Iran said that the newly announced agreement with the United States put an "immediate end" to the countries' war.

items.title: Driver of lorry that crushed Honda City on Karak highway, killing baby girl, arrested after testing positive for meth
items.summary: KUANTAN, June 15 — The lorry driver involved in the accident that claimed four lives at KM43 of the Kuala Lumpur-Karak (KLK) Expressway yesterday has tested positive for drugs. Bentong police chief Supt Zaiham Mohd Kahar said the 40-year-old man was taken to the Bentong district police headquarters for a urine screening test immediately after receiving treatment at the hospital. “The screening test results found that the man was positive for methamphetamine, and he was arrested at about 4am today,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: #NSTviral: Driver claims chased, car struck after brief honk
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A road encounter went viral on Threads after a driver claimed she was chased and her car struck following a brief honk at a motorist said to have encroached into her lane. In a post on Threads, user @whoisfaanyway said she honked briefly as a warning after the pickup truck driver failed to notice her vehicle beside him. "I encountered a road bully today.

items.title: Highland Towers: Final chapter as demolition looms
items.summary: Following the tragedy, residents of Block 2 and Block 3 were ordered to evacuate for safety reasons. The disaster also triggered a prolonged legal dispute involving residents, developers and the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ). In 2006, the Federal Court ruled that 73 residents of Block 2 and Block 3 failed in their claims against MPAJ, while efforts to demolish the remaining two blocks faced years of delays due to legal action and ownership complications.

items.title: Improved market sentiment lifts ringgit at opening
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit extended its upward momentum, opening firmer on Monday against the US dollar and other major currencies, supported by improved risk sentiment following signs of easing geopolitical tensions. At 8am, the local note rose to 4.0430/0500 against the greenback from last Friday's close of 4.0555/0600. "My sense is that the ringgit is moving towards the RM4.00 level against the US dollar.

items.title: Police probe fight between two students at Shah Alam mosque football field
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Police have opened an investigation into a fight involving two school students at a football field near a mosque in Section 19 in Shah Alam on Friday. Shah Alam police chief ACP Sarudin Samah said a report was lodged by the father of one of the students over the incident, which occurred at about 1pm, according to a Berita Harian report today. “One of the students later sought treatment at Shah Alam Hospital and was diagnosed with a soft tissue injury,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Gold gains over 1pct after US, Iran reach peace deal
items.summary: NEW YORK: Gold rose more than one per cent on Monday, after US and Iran officials said they had reached a deal to end their conflict, pushing oil prices lower and easing concerns about inflation and higher interest rates. Spot gold was up 1.8 per cent at US$4,297.42 per ounce, as of 0010 GMT, its highest level since June 9. US gold futures for August delivery rose 1.9 per cent to US$4,318.10.

items.title: Two Indonesian domestic workers placed under consulate protection after alleged abuse in Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The Indonesian Consulate General in Johor Baru (KJRI) has provided protection and assistance to two Indonesian women believed to have been physically abused by their employers in Johor. The consulate said it was alerted to the case on June 13 by YY, who reported alleged physical abuse against herself and two other Indonesian women, YA and SH, who were working as domestic helpers in Johor. “YA travelled to Kuala Lumpur, while YY and SH remained in Johor,” the statement said.

items.title: Baby girl among four killed as skidding trailer crushes Honda City near Bukit Tinggi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Four individuals, including a year-old baby girl, were killed when the car they were in was crushed by a trailer carrying wood dust at Kilometre 43.2 of the East Coast Expressway heading towards Karak yesterday. The Pahang Fire and Rescue Department Operations Centre said that the four, A Halim Hasoon, 55; Rozita Buang, 58; Norbahyah A Halim, 26, and Nayla Afya Aizat, one, died at the scene of the crash between their Honda City and the trailer. “The trailer driver was reported to have been injured and was handed to the Health Ministry for further treatment,” the centre said in a statement today.

items.title: MAIWP highlights holistic development at Darul Kifayah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Students from Kompleks Darul Kifayah brought energy and enthusiasm to their annual sports carnival closing ceremony at Setiawangsa Mini Stadium here on Sunday. "Through sports, students are able to uncover their talents. "This carnival is also held as a closed event among Darul Kifayah students themselves to strengthen the bonds between them."

items.title: Oil prices tumble as US-Iran peace deal paves way for reopening of Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Oil prices fell sharply late on Sunday after an agreement was announced to end the conflict between the US and Iran by mediator Pakistan, Anadolu Ajansi reported. Brent futures, which were trading above US$87, fell to below the US$84 level, down roughly 4 per cent day on day as of 2222 GMT following news of the planned agreement between Washington and Tehran, which is set to be signed on Friday after more than three months of conflict. Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif announced early on Monday that the US and Iran had reached the agreement following intensive negotiations, with both sides declaring an immediate and permanent end to military operations across all fronts, including in Lebanon.

items.title: Markets cheer Iran deal, wait to see oil start flowing
items.summary: NEW YORK: Stocks and bonds rallied and the dollar fell in Asia trade on Monday after the US and Iran agreed a peace deal to re-open the Strait of Hormuz and lift a US blockade on Iran. Here are market analysts' comments on the deal: MRE SPEIZER, MARKET STRATEGIST, WESTPAC, AUCKLAND: "It's positive for risk assets, positive for risky currencies, negative for the US dollar. "I don't think we're going to see US$70 oil too quickly."

items.title: Income-based housing model proposed to replace RM300,000 cap
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia should move away from its RM300,000 affordable housing benchmark and adopt a more locally tailored affordability framework, similar to Singapore's Housing and Development Board (HDB) model, to better address structural mismatches in the property market, experts said. Instead of a fixed price ceiling, Malaysia should adopt a more dynamic affordability formula that takes into account median income, debt servicing ratios and location-specific cost structures, similar to the Singapore model where public housing pricing is closely aligned with household income profiles and long-term financing frameworks. Such an approach could help improve demand alignment, reduce property overhang and ensure that new housing supply better matches actual purchasing power across different income groups and regions.

items.title: From classroom to chaos: Inside Malaysia's PR internship reality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: In lecture halls, communication feels orderly. For students of public relations and strategic communication, internships are not a procedural requirement. When theory meets pressure Interns entering agencies, corporations, government bodies, or media organisations quickly realise they are not observers.

items.title: Two maids abused by employers found safe under Indonesian consulate protection
items.summary: A consulate-general spokesman said both the women, identified only as YY and SH, have been placed in temporary housing pending further assistance. The spokesman said the consular officers were informed of the case on Saturday by YY. The spokesman said YY claimed all three of them were frequently abused by their employers.

items.title: Asean can lead the shift towards a more balanced global financial order
items.summary: Yet this is arguably one of the unique advantages enjoyed by the US through the dominance of the US dollar in global trade finance and foreign exchange reserves. Unlike most other currencies, whose strength is largely derived from economic fundamentals such as productivity, competitiveness and fiscal discipline, the US dollar also benefits from its entrenched position in the global financial system. Such a transition would create a more balanced, resilient and equitable global financial order.

items.title: Who governs the chokepoints?
items.summary: Federalism, Strategic Minerals and the New International Political Economy KUALA LUMPUR: The political debates surrounding oil, gas, rare earths and critical minerals are often presented as disputes over ownership, revenue or jurisdiction. Yet history suggests that resource disputes are rarely about resources alone. This reality is becoming increasingly important as the world enters an era defined not only by geopolitical competition but also by strategic competition over supply chains, technology and critical resources.

items.title: Malaysian homes get smarter
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is emerging as a leading smart living market in Southeast Asia, supported by expanding digital infrastructure and growing public-private collaboration. Samsung Malaysia Electronics president Charles Kim said that as connected devices become more widespread, privacy and cybersecurity remain key considerations. He said Malaysia is contributing to the region's smart living ecosystem.

items.title: Three Czechs die in catamaran, sailboat collision off Croatia
items.summary: ZAGREB: Three Czechs were killed and one was missing after a large passenger catamaran carrying more than 100 people and a sailboat collided in Croatia's central Adriatic on Sunday, officials said. The Czech consul is on site, spokesman Adam Corgo said, offering condolences to the victims' families. Croatian Prime Minister Andrej Plenkovic voiced condolences to the victims' families and his Czech counterpart Andrej Babis.

items.title: [UPDATED] Trailer driver in fatal Karak Highway crash tests positive for drugs
items.summary: BENTONG: The trailer driver involved in a fatal crash that killed four people at Km43 Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway yesterday has tested positive for drugs. District police chief Superintendent Zaiham Mohd Kahar said urine screening confirmed that the suspect had consumed methamphetamine. He said the driver was brought to the Bentong police headquarters after undergoing treatment at Bentong Hospital.

items.title: Shah Alam cops probe fight between two students at football field
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police are investigating a fight involving two school students at a mosque football field in Section 19 here last Friday. District police chief assistant commissioner Sarudin Samah said police received a report from the father of one of the students at about 1pm. "The altercation involved only the two students but is believed to have been witnessed by several other students at the scene.

items.title: G7 protest turns from carnival to violent stand-off
items.summary: GENEVA: An anti-G7 protest in Geneva began in a carnival atmosphere on Sunday before descending into violence, with building facades smashed in and police firing tear gas at masked youths. The day started in tranquil fashion: ahead of the march, people gathered in a park overlooking Lake Geneva, seeking shade from the blazing afternoon sun and catching up with friends as they arrived. Geneva's police said around 20,000 people attended; the vast majority were well behaved and not involved in any violence.

items.title: US to release US$12bil in frozen assets
items.summary: TEHERAN: The United States will release US$12 billion in frozen assets to Iran before the start of negotiations, Mehr news agency reported on Monday, quoting a 14-point memorandum of understanding between the two nations. The document published by Mehr stipulated "the release of US$24 billion in frozen Iranian assets during the 60-day negotiation period" that begins after the memorandum of understanding is concluded. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Iran military says it 'humiliated' US, Israel in war
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's military said on Monday it had "humiliated" the United States and Israel, in a statement issued after an agreement to immediately end the conflict was announced. Iranian forces "have, through the imposition of their divine and iron will upon the humiliated American and Zionist enemies, demonstrated with strength that the enemy has no path other than accepting defeat and surrender", the general staff said in a statement broadcast by state television. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysia targets landfill reliance cut as food waste crisis deepens [WATCH]
items.summary: The agency said a vital part of this strategy is the reduction of food waste. "Food waste makes up 40.03 per cent of the 36,900 tonnes of solid waste generated daily in the country. SWCorp said that in 2025, licensed collection companies sent 7,125.77 tonnes of food waste for treatment, a significant jump from the 4,536 tonnes in 2024.

items.title: Demand for food aid jumps 40pct as living costs bite [WATCH]
items.summary: RISING living costs have triggered a surge in demand for food aid, with The Lost Food Project (TLFP) reporting a 35 per cent to 40 per cent increase in the number of families seeking help between 2025 and 2026. TLFP provides food aid for more than 36,000 B40 families across 180 locations, including people's housing projects (PPRs), civil society groups and various charities. TLFP general manager Stuart Galvin said demand from low-income communities has continued to rise sharply.

items.title: Israeli settlers torch West Bank vehicles, mosque
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israeli settlers torched vehicles and attempted to set fire to a mosque in the occupied West Bank on Sunday, the Palestinian news agency reported, as the Israeli military deployed troops to quell what it described as violent riots by "Israeli civilians". According to the official Palestinian news agency Wafa, settlers carried out two attacks in the West Bank late on Sunday. The Israeli military confirmed it had dispatched forces to multiple locations in the West Bank following reports of arson and riots.

items.title: Iran foresees US talks within two months on 'final agreement'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran said it would hold talks with the United States within two months to seek a "final agreement" on settling the countries' war, after they announced an deal to immediately halt the conflict. "When negotiations begin within 60 days with the aim of reaching a final agreement, if such a final agreement is ultimately achieved, the key issue for us will naturally be the implementation of its provisions," Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said in televised comments early Monday. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Malaysians throw away 6,000 tonnes of edible food daily [WATCH]
items.summary: Leading the charge against this is The Lost Food Project (TLFP), a pioneer food bank working tirelessly to prevent wastage and ensure that needy communities receive food aid. TLFP general manager Stuart Galvin said between January 2025 and May 2026, the organisation rescued approximately 4.36 million kilogrammes of food. "Cumulatively, since our inception in 2016 to January 2026, TLFP has rescued over 11 million kilogrammes of food and delivered 33 million meals."

items.title: Marine police seize over 5,000 litres of diesel in Terengganu raid, arrest 55-year-old man
items.summary: CHUKAI, June 14 — The Marine Police Region Three seized 5,150 litres of diesel in a raid at a premises in Hulu Jabor near here yesterday. Marine Police Region Three commander ACP Mohd Khairuddin Jamal said the seizure was carried out after the premises were suspected of illegally storing and handling diesel during a raid at 11 am. Mohd Khairuddin said his team also arrested a 55-year-old man to assist in the investigation.

items.title: Drone classrooms could be key to Malaysia’s STEM push, says science minister
items.summary: TANJONG MALIM, June 14 — Drone technology has unique advantages that can attract students to pursue studies in Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics (STEM), Science, Technology and Innovation Minister Datuk Chang Lih Kang said. He said a more interactive and engaging drone-based learning approach could help make STEM subjects more accessible to students while encouraging them to explore science and technology-related knowledge. “This is a model that we believe can succeed in developing STEM, STEAM (Science, Technology, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics) and STREAM (Science, Technology, Reading/Writing, Engineering, Arts and Mathematics) talents among young people, making them more interested in science, technology, engineering, mathematics and related fields,” he told reporters after officiating the STEM Room at SJKT Ladang Katoyang here today.

items.title: Jakim confirms fake halal cert in chicken seizure at Bukit Kayu Hitam
items.summary: BUKIT KAYU HITAM, June 14 — The Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) seized 26,040 kilogrammes of frozen chicken worth RM208,320 on June 8 over suspicions that a fake halal certificate was used for its importation. Bukit Kayu Hitam AKPS commander SAC Fauzi Mohd Yusof said the seizure was conducted jointly with officers from the Malaysian Quarantine and Inspection Services Department (Maqis) at the Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) Complex here following the submission of an import declaration by the importing agent for inspection and verification purposes. He said initial checks found irregularities in the halal certificate submitted, as the information in the system did not match the details on the certificate.

items.title: When wildlife and humans collide in Malaysia: Losses near RM60m in four years
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, June 14 — A total of 76,361 wildlife conflict complaints involving estimated losses of RM58.7 million were recorded nationwide between 2021 and May this year, Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said. Arthur said human-wildlife conflicts involving macaques, elephants, wild boars, Malayan tigers and tapirs were showing an increasingly worrying trend. Arthur said the ministry had undertaken various initiatives to address human-wildlife conflicts, particularly those involving elephants, including the implementation of the National Elephant Conservation Action Plan (NECAP), the establishment of elephant sanctuaries and the National Elephant Conservation Centre (NECC) in Lanchang, Pahang, as well as the installation of elephant electric fencing systems.

items.title: ETS back on track after power outage, but eight services running late
items.summary: However, Keretapi Tanah Melayu Berhad (KTMB) in a statement today said that several ETS services on the KL Sentral–JB Sentral–KL Sentral route are still experiencing temporary delays to enable the operation schedule to be stabilised. KTMB said as of 3.30 pm today, eight ETS services are still experiencing delays, namely train EX9573 which is three hours and 34 minutes late, EP9524 (two hours and 35 minutes), EP9326 (one hour and 40 minutes), EP9428 (25 minutes). Also experiencing delays are trains EP9323 (two hours and 11 minutes), EG9343 (one hour and 14 minutes), EG9442 (two hours and 51 minutes) and EX9575 (one hour).

items.title: Maybe just stay home tonight: Storms sweeping Malaysia until 9pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning forecasting thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds across almost all states and Kuala Lumpur until 9pm today. MetMalaysia, in a warning issued at 4.35pm, said adverse weather conditions are expected in Perlis, Penang, Perak and Kuala Lumpur. Other states expected to be affected are Kedah (Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Pokok Sena, Padang Terap, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu), Kelantan (Gua Musang), and Pahang (Cameron Highlands, Lipis, Raub, Jerantut, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin).

items.title: Sabah doesn’t just need films, it needs a film culture, says award-winning filmmaker Nadira Ilana amid industry brain drain
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 14 — Award-winning Malaysian filmmaker Nadira Ilana (pic) said Sabah’s creative industry was deliberately set back by the National Cultural Policy of 1971, stripping Sabahans of the cultural and linguistic freedoms needed to build a thriving arts sector. “The creative industries in Sabah was ‘sabotaged’. Sabahans were making our own documentaries in the 1970s,” she said at the Daily Express Creative Minds Forum 2026, to spotlight and celebrate the voices shaping Sabah’s creative landscape.

items.title: Where’s that umbrella: Thunderstorms, heavy rainfall expected all over Malaysia till up to 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in almost all states apart from Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya until 6pm today. MetMalaysia in its warning at 2.25 pm said the weather conditions are expected to occur throughout the states of Perak, Selangor, Kuala Lumpur and Putrajaya. In Kedah, the areas affected are Kuala Muda, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu, while in Penang it includes Seberang Perai Utara, Seberang Perai Tengah and Seberang Perai Selatan.

items.title: ‘Good luck’: Khaled responds to former Umno figures joining PAS
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin responded with a terse “good luck” when asked about the decision by several former party figures to join PAS. Speaking after a working visit to the 4th Royal Artillery Regiment at Segamat Camp today, Khaled, who is also defence minister, said former minister Datuk Mohd Zaid Ibrahim had left Umno long ago and that his decision to join PAS was not unexpected. Among those introduced were Zaid; former Negeri Sembilan executive councillor and former Ampangan assemblyman Datuk Mohamad Rafie Abdul Malek; Rahimi Osman, a former private secretary to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim; and former Perak executive councillor and former Bersatu vice-president Datuk Hamidah Othman.

items.title: Six Madani recreational parks in Penang to be completed by year-end
items.summary: State Local Government, Town and Country Planning Committee chairman Jason H’ng Mooi Lye said three projects under the Penang Island City Council (MBPP) have commenced implementation, while another three under the Seberang Perai City Council (MBSP) are in the final stage of the quotation process. “The three projects under MBPP are the upgrading of the Jalan Cheeseman neighbourhood park in Jelutong, Jalan Kampung Bukit neighbourhood park in Bayan Lepas, and Lengkok Merbah 3 neighbourhood park, also in Bayan Lepas. For Seberang Perai, he said the projects involve landscape upgrading works at open spaces in Lorong Pauh 2, Taman Pauh and Jalan Kelisa Emas 4, Taman Kelisa in Seberang Perai Tengah, as well as Lorong Belatuk 3/Pekeliling Belatuk, Taman Belatuk in Nibong Tebal, Seberang Perai Selatan.

items.title: PTPTN allocates RM3b for higher education financing this year
items.summary: TASEK GELUGOR, June 14 — The National Higher Education Fund Corporation (PTPTN) has allocated about RM3 billion this year to support students pursuing higher education at institutions of higher learning. Its chairman Datuk Seri Norliza Abdul Rahim said the allocation, channelled through PTPTN education loans, is aimed at ensuring no student is deprived of higher education due to financial constraints. Norliza said PTPTN also provides an Advance Loan Payment (WPP) of RM1,500 to help new students meet initial expenses before registering at their institutions.

items.title: Police seek public’s help to trace missing tahfiz student in Ampang
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are seeking the public’s assistance to trace a 15-year-old boy who was reported missing after he was believed to have left a tahfiz school in Ulu Kelang, Ampang, on Friday. Ampang Jaya Police chief ACP Khairul Anuar Khalid said teachers at the school discovered that Muhammad Daniel Azizan Abdullah was missing and contacted his mother at 8.15 pm on Friday. Anyone with information on his whereabouts is urged to contact investigating officer Insp Nur Alia Dyana Raimi at 017-6515201, or the Ampang Jaya Police headquarters control centre at 03-42897222, or the nearest police station.

items.title: El Nino could bring heat, haze and water shortages into 2027, MetMalaysia warns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Malaysia is expected to experience hotter and drier conditions in the coming months as the El Niño phenomenon is forecast to begin influencing the country’s weather patterns, potentially lasting until early next year, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said today. The department warned that the conditions could reduce rainfall in several areas, increasing the risk of water shortages, forest and peatland fires, as well as haze episodes. “As a preparedness measure, MetMalaysia has strengthened the dissemination of weather information and early warnings to the government, relevant agencies and the public,” the statement said.

items.title: Blood, sweat and tears — and lost cash: KL police tracing mule accounts in BTS ticket scam probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Police are tracing several bank accounts believed to have been used as mule accounts in connection with alleged fraud involving the sale of tickets for BTS’ upcoming concert in Malaysia. According to Berita Harian , Brickfields district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hoo Chang Hook said investigations were ongoing and several bank accounts suspected of being involved had already been identified. Hoo said no new police reports had been lodged in relation to the case so far.

items.title: Police identify two bodies found in Kedah River, rule out link between deaths
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 14 — Police have identified one of two men whose bodies were recovered from the Kedah River today as a 16-year-old boy who had been reported missing two days earlier, adding that there was no indication the deaths were linked. Harian Metro reported Kota Setar police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali as saying that the teenager, identified as Saw Chee Wan, was believed to have left his home on June 12. Although the two bodies were found about 100 metres apart, police said investigations showed the cases were unrelated.

items.title: Drones more effective than boat patrols along Sungai Golok, say Kelantan police
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 14 — The use of drones is more effective than conventional boat patrols in curbing smuggling activities along the Sungai Golok border, as syndicates can easily detect patrol boats operating in the narrow river, Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said. He said the movement and engine noise of Marine Police patrol boats can be heard from across the border, allowing smugglers to suspend their activities before enforcement personnel can take action. Mohd Yusoff said 300 General Operations Force (GOF) personnel are deployed each month to monitor the 91-kilometre border stretch through 17 control posts.

items.title: Court ruling clears way for Highland Towers demolition before year end, says MPAJ
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The remaining blocks of the Highland Towers condominium in Hulu Kelang are expected to be demolished before the end of the year, following a Magistrates’ Court decision allowing the Ampang Jaya Municipal Council (MPAJ) to proceed with the long-delayed plan. MPAJ deputy president Hasrolnizam Shaari said the court approved the council’s application last week, paving the way for the demolition of the abandoned structures that have stood vacant for more than three decades. Within this year, the demolition process will take place,” he told Harian Metro today.

items.title: Selangor Bomba: Four women rescued after losing their way on Bukit Tunggul hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Four women who lost their way while hiking at Bukit Tunggul in Dengkil were found safe and escorted out by firefighters today. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ahmad Mukhlis Mukhtar said authorities received an emergency call at 10.13am reporting that the group had become disoriented during the hike. “All four women were located by firefighters and escorted to a safe area.

items.title: Two bodies found 100m apart in Kedah River near Alor Setar bridge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — The bodies of two males, aged 16 and 46, were found in the Kedah River near the Jeragan Waterfront Bridge in Alor Setar today. Kedah Fire and Rescue Department Zone 1 chief Ahmad Aminuddin Abd Rahim said firefighters were first alerted at 8.45am after receiving a report of a body caught among debris in the river. Following checks carried out with police officers, firefighters retrieved the second body from the river.

items.title: Zahid says new Malay parties must unite, not divide
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said new Malay-based political parties were welcome, provided they helped unite rather than further divide the community. Speaking after the Majlis Zikir Peringatan Haul Asmaulhusna 2026 at Masjid Sultan Iskandar in Bandar Dato’ Onn, Johor Bahru, today, the Barisan Nasional chairman said any individual or political figure was free to establish a new party as long as it helped unite Malays rather than deepen existing divisions. “Whatever platform is used, if in the end we are able to unite our actions around the national agenda, the Islamic agenda and the agenda of our community, insya-Allah.

items.title: Rosmah denies role in alleged US$13m property deal, lodges police report over online claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 14 — Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has denied allegations linking her and her family to an alleged US$13 million (RM53 million) property transaction, saying she had lodged a police report and would pursue legal action against those responsible for disseminating the claims online. In a statement issued today in response to videos circulating on social media, the wife of former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak said she had no involvement, either directly or indirectly, in the acquisition, transfer or ownership of any luxury property, including one allegedly valued at US$13 million. Rosmah said the videos had harmed her family’s reputation.

items.title: Two married couples held over viral video of domestic helper assault, says Johor police chief
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 14 — Two married couples were arrested yesterday to assist in investigations after a video showing domestic helpers being assaulted in Taman Johor near Tampoi went viral on social media. Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the couples, aged between 30 and 34, were arrested at a house in the residential area at about 7.30 pm by personnel from the Johor Criminal Investigation Department (CID) and the Johor Bahru Utara district police headquarters. Meanwhile, Ab Rahaman said police had seized mobile phones, clothing belonging to the suspects, a closed-circuit television (CCTV) camera and the two domestic helpers’ passports.
